title: A Big Company Gets a Huge Offer to Buy It
summary: Universal Music Group is a very big music company that works with famous singers like Taylor Swift. A company called Pershing Square wants to buy Universal for a lot of money, about $64 billion. Pershing Square is an investment company that already owns part of Universal and other big companies like Google. The boss of Pershing Square, Bill Ackman, says Universal is a great company with many artists and does good business. He thinks buying Universal will help fix some problems, like its stock price not going up as much as it should. If the deal happens, Universal's owners will get cash and shares in a new company listed in New York. The deal might finish by the end of the year, and Universal's stock went up when people heard the news.

questions.question: What happened to Universal's stock when people heard the news?

background_read: Universal Music Group is one of the biggest music companies in the world. It helps singers and bands make and sell their music. Companies like Pershing Square invest money in other companies to try to make more money. When a company is bought, it means someone else takes control of it. Stock prices show how much people think a company is worth. Deals like this can change how companies work and affect the music we listen to.
Article_Structure: WHO: Universal Music Group and Pershing Square, with Bill Ackman as the boss.
WHAT: Pershing Square offers to buy Universal Music Group for $64 billion.
WHERE: The new company would be listed in New York, USA.
WHY: To fix problems like Universal's stock price and help it grow.

perspectives.description: Buying Universal could make Pershing Square more money and help Universal improve.
